operating_state: Revenue reached $26.91 billion, up 61% year-over-year, driven by strong demand for NVIDIA Ampere architecture products across Graphics and Compute & Networking segments. Graphics revenue increased 61% primarily from GeForce GPU sales benefiting from RTX 30 Series ramp and cryptocurrency mining capability. Compute & Networking revenue increased 61% driven by hyperscale customer sales for cloud computing and AI workloads, plus full-year networking revenue inclusion following Mellanox acquisition.
cost_structure: Gross margin expanded 260 basis points to 64.9% primarily due to elimination of Mellanox inventory step-up charge from prior year and higher-end mix within Graphics, partially offset by product mix shift within Compute & Networking. Operating expenses increased 27% driven by stock-based compensation, employee growth-related compensation costs, and higher infrastructure expenses. Manufacturing lead times extended to twelve months or longer, requiring advance inventory commitments and premium payments to secure supply capacity with inventory provisions totaling $354 million creating 0.9% unfavorable gross margin impact.
material_events: NVIDIA and SoftBank terminated the Arm acquisition agreement on February 8, 2022, due to regulatory challenges, resulting in a planned $1.36 billion charge in Q1 fiscal 2023 reflecting write-off of the prepayment. The company experienced a security incident on February 23, 2022, involving unauthorized network access by criminal threat actors who obtained proprietary information including source code. NVIDIA issued debt during fiscal 2022 and ceased product sales to Russia following sanctions and export restrictions.
macro_exposures: Starting fiscal year 2023, the Tax Cuts and Jobs Act requires capitalization and amortization of research and development expenditures over five to fifteen years, materially reducing cash flows and significantly increasing cash tax payments unless Congress modifies the provision. Geopolitical tensions in regions where product manufacturing is concentrated could disrupt operations and supply chains, while U.S. and foreign government trade restrictions create compliance risks.
forward_outlook: Management expects to remain supply-constrained into the first half of fiscal 2023 with planned capital investments and maintains sufficient liquidity through cash and marketable securities. The company plans to implement new ERP system functionality in Q1 fiscal 2023 and targets sourcing renewable energy by end of fiscal 2025. Management expects continued investment in AI capabilities across products and services, though increasing regulatory focus on AI technologies may create additional restrictions.
uncertainty_profile: Long manufacturing lead times extending twelve months create significant supply-demand mismatch risks and potential excess inventory exposure, while cryptocurrency mining demand remains unpredictable with limited visibility into actual GPU demand impact from pending Ethereum transitions. Unrecognized tax benefits remain with uncertain timing for resolution, while the company faces ongoing tax audits across multiple jurisdictions. Evolving data privacy regulations and pending European AI regulation could impose compliance obligations requiring business practice changes.
